name: Pilar de la Media Legua – circular from Trigueros via Beas
name_original: Pilar de la Media Legua – circular desde Trigueros por Beas
region_intro: Hiking around Trigueros offers access to diverse natural features and historical attractions within the Huelva province of Spain. The region is characterized by varied landscapes, including accessible paths through the Andalusian countryside, and is notable for its significant megalithic monuments. Trails often traverse areas with diverse ecosystems, providing a range of experiences for outdoor enthusiasts. Best hiking trails around Trigueros The most popular hiking route is Fog - circular tour to the walled city, a 4.2 miles (6.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 48 minutes to complete. This easy route is suitable for all skill levels. Another top favourite among local hikers is Dolmen de Soto Loop from Niebla, a moderate 10.8 miles (17.4 km) path. This route explores the ancient Dolmen de Soto, a significant megalithic monument, and its surrounding lands. Local hikers also love the Pilar de la Media Legua – circular from Trigueros via Beas, a 11.9 miles (19.2 km) trail leading through the Andalusian countryside, often completed in about 4 hours 58 minutes. Hiking around Trigueros is defined by accessible paths, megalithic monuments, and diverse natural areas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Trigueros are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.2 stars from 4 reviews. More than 20 hikers have used komoot to explore Trigueros's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Trigueros?
A: Trigueros offers a good selection of hiking opportunities, with over 15 trails to explore. These routes cater to various preferences, from easy strolls to more challenging treks through the Andalusian countryside.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Trigueros?
A: Yes, Trigueros has several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983673949/fog-circular-tour-to-the-walled-city" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fog - circular tour to the walled city&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.2 miles (6.8 km) long and generally takes less than two hours to complete. This route is suitable for all skill levels.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on hikes around Trigueros?
A: Hiking around Trigueros allows you to experience diverse natural landscapes typical of the Huelva province. You'll find accessible paths winding through the Andalusian countryside, with varied ecosystems. The wider region is known for its marshes, beaches, and forests, offering a rich natural backdrop for your walks.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Trigueros area?
A: Yes, many routes around Trigueros are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. A notable circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984106961/dolmen-de-soto-loop-from-niebla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Soto Loop from Niebla&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 10.8-mile (17.4 km) path exploring the ancient megalithic monument and its surroundings.

Q: What historical landmarks or attractions can I visit while hiking near Trigueros?
A: Trigueros is rich in historical significance. The most prominent landmark is the &lt;strong&gt;Dolmen de Soto&lt;/strong&gt;, an impressive megalithic monument dating back to 3000-2500 BC. You can explore this ancient site via the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984106961/dolmen-de-soto-loop-from-niebla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Soto Loop from Niebla&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, nearby attractions include the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4525837" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Niebla Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/386978" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge 🌉 Niebla&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Trigueros?
A: The Huelva province, where Trigueros is located, enjoys a mild climate for much of the year. This makes it an ideal destination for hiking across different seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable temperatures for extended walks.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging hikes available for experienced hikers?
A: For those seeking a greater challenge, Trigueros and its surroundings offer longer routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984174176/moguer-palos-de-la-frontera-circular-by-the-rio-tinto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moguer - Palos de la Frontera – circular by the Río Tinto&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 13.4-mile (21.5 km) trail, providing a more demanding experience.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Trigueros?
A: The hiking routes in Trigueros are well-regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 3.2 stars from 4 reviews. Hikers often praise the accessible paths and the diverse natural areas that make for an enjoyable outdoor experience.

Q: Can I find trails that connect to larger hiking networks from Trigueros?
A: Yes, Trigueros is connected to broader hiking initiatives. It serves as a gateway to routes like the "Huelva por la TransAndalus" natural routes and the "Camino Xacobeo Sur Huelva-Zafra," which is part of the Camino de Santiago. These connections offer opportunities for longer, more extensive treks beyond the immediate area.

Q: Are there any trails that feature reservoirs or water bodies?
A: Yes, you can find routes that incorporate water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1131741495/campanario-reservoir-via-verde-de-los-molinos-del-agua-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campanario Reservoir - Vía Verde de los Molinos del Agua Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8-mile (12.9 km) hike that takes you around the reservoir, offering scenic views and a different natural environment.

Q: What is the typical duration for a moderate hike in Trigueros?
A: Moderate hikes in Trigueros can vary in length and duration. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984116323/pilar-de-la-media-legua-circular-from-trigueros-via-beas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pilar de la Media Legua – circular from Trigueros via Beas&lt;/a&gt; is an 11.9-mile (19.2 km) trail that typically takes around 4 hours and 58 minutes to complete, offering a substantial but manageable outing.
tips: This typical town in the interior of Huelva offers several tourist alternatives despite its small size. With several trails to choose from, you can visit the Candoncillo Reservoir or the neighboring towns of Trigueros following the Camino de Santiago del Sur.

It owes its name to the distance that separates it from the town of Trigueros, this trough is a clear example of Roman engineering for the transport of water. It is located on the old Roman road that linked Seville with Ayamonte and dates from the 2nd century, a unique and admirable example.
